picks.reason: Melton has only 25.2 AGG IP with weak peripherals (4.82 SIERA, 7.8% K-BB%, Stf+ 97) and a [sm] L14 — never anchors a play. Newcomb's 2.76 ERA is reliever-deployment-flavored. TT prices flagged stale so totals market is absent, and my number sits near the 9.0 line anyway. No clean read either way.
picks.data_gap: Melton full-sample stabilization; non-stale total price.

picks.reason: Warren's AGG SIERA 3.76/16.7% K-BB% is solid, and CIN's 89 wRC+ vs RHP is weak, but the market has NYY priced fully at -205 — no edge in laying that. NYY lineup not posted, so no 3u read possible. My xR lands near 9.0 vs posted 9.5 (Over price stale), under my gate. No actionable spot.
picks.data_gap: NYY confirmed lineup; non-stale Over price.

picks.reason: Two weak arms (Corbin 4.75 SIERA, Rea 5.00 SIERA with CHC T1 pen 0/2 fresh) point Over, but lineups unconfirmed and TT prices flagged stale — totals market absent. My xR ~9.4 vs 9.5 is inside the gate regardless. No staked play.

Two strong arms (Buehler AGG 4.05 SIERA but trending up, Eovaldi 3.59 SIERA/17.1% K-BB%) in a pitcher-friendly park (Runs 83, roof likely closed 92), both pens rested. My xR builds to ~7.0 vs posted 7.5. That's a 0.5-run Under lean — real but below my 0.8 gate, so zero stake, logged for CLV.

Two ace-level lefties (Harrison 3.11 SIERA, Sale 3.09 SIERA/116 Stf+) and two elite bullpens point Under, but MIL bats a 136 wRC+ vs LHP and ATL pen is fully fresh while MIL's T3 is 0/5. My xR ~7.0 lands right on the posted 7.0. No edge. Price on either ML reflects a true coinflip-ish game with no perception gap.

McDonald has only 42.2 AGG IP with a 6.2 BB/9 L14 [sm] command flag — never anchors. Meyer's 2.75 ERA sits well above his 3.67 SIERA, a mild regression flag, but MIA offense is league-average vs RHP. My xR ~7.9 vs 8.0 is inside the gate. Under TT price flagged stale. No play.
picks.data_gap: Confirmed lineups; non-stale Under price; roof status.

picks.reason: Seymour has only 6.0 AGG IP and 38.1 thin season IP — a small-sample arm that caps any total at LEAN per my fade rules. Cavalli is solid (3.71 SIERA, 16.8% K-BB%) but lineups unconfirmed. Trop is a HR park (119) but TB offense is muted (5.3% Brl). My xR ~7.9 vs 8.0, no edge.

Arrighetti's 2.57 ERA vs 4.56 AGG SIERA is a classic regression flag I'd lean against, but CLE's offense is ice-cold (L10RS 2.9, 84 wRC+ vs LHP issue is moot — they're RHP-side). Cantillo AGG 4.62 SIERA is mediocre. My xR ~8.3 vs 8.5, inside gate. The ERA-darling regression isn't enough vs a weak CLE bat to justify HOU side value at -143.

Sánchez is genuinely elite (2.66 SIERA, 24.3% K-BB%, 118 Stf+) and NYM bats only 92 wRC+ vs LHP; Peralta is solid (4.11 SIERA). My xR builds to ~6.9 vs posted 7.5 even in a hitter park (104 Runs). That's a 0.6-run Under lean — below my 0.8 gate, zero stake. PHI ML is fully priced at -189; no side edge.

Coors — automatic LEAN cap on totals per my fade rules; variance too high to stake either way. Skenes is dominant (2.83 SIERA) and would normally drag a total down, but Sugano (5.18 SIERA, 80 Stf+) plus altitude plus 16 mph wind out makes the number untrustworthy. PIT ML fully priced at -210; no side value.

Ureña's 2.60 ERA vs 4.60 AGG SIERA (9.3% K-BB%, 5.2 BB/9 L14) is a strong regression-fade signal, and Ginn (4.27 SIERA, 91 Stf+) is also mediocre in a hitter park (121 Runs). Both point Over, but my xR ~9.6 vs posted 9.5 is essentially on the line — no run-gap edge. Extreme park caps confidence anyway.

This is my favorite spot type — Gallen's 5.35 ERA looks ugly but his 4.73 AGG SIERA is the real number, while Bradley (3.94 SIERA, 108 Stf+) is the better arm and is priced as the underdog/near-pick. The market already has this near pick'em (+111/-130), so the perception gap is mostly priced. My side edge on Bradley computes to ~3 pts, under my 4.0 gate. xR ~8.9 vs 9.0, no total edge.

Yamamoto (3.31 SIERA, 20.1% K-BB%) is excellent and T.Rogers (5.86 ERA / 4.81 SIERA) is weak, but LAD is fully priced at -261 — no side value, and the -1.5 RL at -121 doesn't beat my fair number. BAL bats 124 wRC+ vs RHP keeps the Under honest; my xR ~7.8 vs 8.0, inside gate. No play.

T-Mobile is the most pitcher-friendly park here (Runs 86), both offenses are weak (BOS 92 wRC+ vs RHP, SEA 79 vs LHP), and Hancock (3.59 SIERA) is fine despite an ugly L14 [sm]. Early's 5.8 BB/9 L14 command flag adds some Over risk. My xR ~7.0 vs 7.5, but Under price at -125 shades my gate up to 1.0 — a 0.5-run lean stays zero stake.
